name: Buttonbush
summary: A pollinator-magnet wetland shrub of pond margins and swale bottoms. White spherical flower heads in June–July draw native bees, honey bees, butterflies, sphinx moths, and hummingbirds in numbers rarely seen on any other PA native.
notes: Native throughout PA in permanently wet sites. Tolerates up to 3 feet of standing water. Nutlets feed 25+ species of waterfowl and songbirds (wood ducks especially). Larval host for Hydrangea Sphinx and Titan Sphinx moths. Will not survive upland — it needs wet feet.

refs.label: PSU Extension — Native Shrubs for Pollinators

planting.aspect: Pond edges; swale bottoms; permanent wet hollows
planting.moisture: Wet to inundated; tolerates 3 ft standing water
planting.site_notes: Permanently wet site only — will not survive upland. No deer protection needed; deer largely ignore it. Plant in clusters of 3–5 for visual mass and reliable pollination. Tolerates flooding for extended periods.
